title: Philosophy and ethics
explanation:
Philosophy and ethics

  • morality, autonomy, utilitarianism, dilemma, conscience
  • virtue, obligation, consent, integrity, impartiality
  • verbs: justify, condemn, uphold, compromise

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The documentary explores a classic moral ____ faced by doctors. | *dilemma | receipt | platform
Respect for patient ____ is central to medical ethics. | *autonomy | economy | anatomy
She acted according to her ____ and resigned. | *conscience | contract | campaign
Judges are expected to remain ____. | *impartial | impatient | informal

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Critics ____ the decision as deeply unfair. | condemn
The committee tried to ____ a difficult compromise. | reach
He refused to ____ his principles for profit. | compromise
The court must ____ the law even when it is unpopular. | uphold
Can violence ever be ____ in self-defence? | justified

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Informed ____ is required before the procedure. | *consent | content | comment
Professional ____ matters in public office. | *integrity | industry | inflation
They debated whether pleasure should define ____.  | *morality | mobility | majority
Courage is often described as a moral ____.  | *virtue | verdict | vaccine
